title: SaaS Assignment Source Evidence
purpose: Prove the daemon-side SaaS assignment source/reporter contract without claiming control-plane or client presentation ownership.

facts.name: shared assignment contract
facts.summary: SaaS adapter consumes assignment DTO/state/event constants from riido-contracts.

facts.name: poll start active cancel mapping
facts.summary: Poll start/active become TaskRequest; poll cancel is delivered to the local cancellation watcher.

facts.name: long poll wait contract
facts.summary: PollRequest.wait_ms defaults to 30 seconds and extends the HTTP timeout.

facts.name: candidate backpressure
facts.summary: Multiple candidate agents are short-polled first and only one candidate receives the long poll.

facts.name: runtime wave fairness
facts.summary: A runtime claim wave does not let an earlier runtime long-poll block later runtimes; only the final runtime receives the long-poll hint.

facts.name: stale binding recovery
facts.summary: A stale dynamic binding poll rejection invalidates the binding cache and does not abort other live candidates in the same claim wave.

facts.name: heartbeat stale cancellation
facts.summary: Daemon forwards active assignment ids and cancels local watchers when heartbeat response omits a requested assignment.

facts.name: events endpoint owns progress and lifecycle reports
facts.summary: Progress, lifecycle, running, session pin, terminal result, and coalesced text body reports are posted to /events.

facts.name: provider limit failure category
facts.summary: Provider token, quota, rate-limit, and cloud-credit exhaustion failures are reported with provider_limit metadata for control-plane/client normalization.

facts.name: auth forwarding
facts.summary: Device credentials and bearer token, when configured, are attached to SaaS HTTP requests.

facts.name: dynamic runtime bindings
facts.summary: Dynamic /v1/daemon/agent-bindings are cached and used to map runtime/provider scope to agent ids.

boundaries.name: control-plane stale deadline
boundaries.summary: The 20-second stale projection is upstream; daemon evidence only proves it honors omitted heartbeat refreshes.

boundaries.name: task-thread presentation
boundaries.summary: Figma task-thread copy, cold collection rows, and busy/deleted agent messages are not daemon responsibilities.

boundaries.name: agent authorization policy
boundaries.summary: Public/private agent access policy is evaluated before daemon receives assignment snapshots.

boundaries.name: private infra and stores
boundaries.summary: HTTP handlers, persistence, SSE fan-out, metrics, health read models, Terraform, and secrets are not daemon-owned.

absent_surfaces.name: thread-progress endpoint ownership
absent_surfaces.reason: daemon reports progress through /events and must not claim the cold task-thread endpoint

absent_surfaces.name: client presentation copy ownership
absent_surfaces.reason: client/control-plane own Figma copy and task-thread presentation

absent_surfaces.name: private server package import
absent_surfaces.reason: public daemon must not import private control-plane implementation

assertions: Implemented facts must point at local source evidence.
Upstream/client boundaries must not be described as daemon-owned behavior.
Reader docs must be generated from this manifest.
The daemon must not claim /thread-progress ownership while source code posts to /events.
